title: RFP KB Canonical Entry v2
description: Schema for knowledge base entries. v2 is a superset of v1 — all new fields have defaults so existing v1 entries remain valid.

kb_id.description: Unique identifier. v1 format: 'kb_0001' or 'wms_0001'. v2 format: '{PREFIX}-{CAT}-{NNNN}' e.g. 'WMS-FUNC-0042'

id.description: v2 canonical ID. Format: {PREFIX}-{CAT}-{NNNN}. Derived from family id_prefix + category code + sequence number.

domain.description: Product family domain key. Must match a key in family_config.json.

family_code.description: Same as domain. Used for ChromaDB filtered queries. Kept separate for future multi-family entries.

scope.description: Whether this applies to the whole BY platform or is product-specific.

subcategory.description: Finer-grained category. Free text — use consistent names within a domain.

canonical_question.description: The primary, clean phrasing of the question. Used as the main embedding text.

question_variants.description: Alternative phrasings of the same question. Improves RAG recall by covering different customer wordings.

canonical_answer.description: The authoritative answer. Should be self-contained — assume no other context.

solution_codes.description: Which specific solution codes this entry applies to. Subset of the family's solution_codes in family_config.json. Empty = applies to all solutions in the family.

tags.description: Keyword tags for search boosting and filtering. Lowercase, no spaces.

source_rfps.description: Which RFP deals or files contributed to this answer. Used for traceability.

cloud_native_only.description: If true, this answer only applies to the cloud-native / SaaS deployment. On-premise customers should be flagged.

versioning.description: Product version applicability. Inherited from v1 schema.

rich_metadata.description: Extended metadata. Superset of v1 rich_metadata.

search_blob.description: Pre-built search string combining all searchable fields. Auto-generated — do not edit manually.

created_date.description: ISO date when entry was created.

examples.canonical_question: Does Blue Yonder WMS support wave-based picking?
question_variants: Can Blue Yonder WMS do wave picking?
What picking strategies does WMS support?
Is wave picking available in BY WMS?
examples.canonical_answer: Yes, Blue Yonder WMS supports wave-based picking as well as waveless and dynamic picking strategies. Waves can be configured by order type, carrier, route, or other criteria.
examples.search_blob: DOMAIN: wms | SCOPE: product_specific || CAT: functional / core_process || Q: Does Blue Yonder WMS support wave-based picking? || VARIANTS: Can Blue Yonder WMS do wave picking? | What picking strategies does WMS support? || TAGS: picking, wave, fulfillment || A: Yes, Blue Yonder WMS supports wave-based picking...
